content.explanation:
**Has To / Have To — External Obligation (बाहरी ज़रूरत):**

"Have to / Has to" = करना पड़ता है / करना होता है
यह external obligation है — rules, circumstances, या someone else की requirement से।

**Have To vs Must:**
• Must = internal obligation (मैं खुद feel करता हूँ)
• Have to = external obligation (कोई rule या situation force करती है)

**Structure:**
• I/We/You/They → **have to** + V1
• He/She/It → **has to** + V1

🇮🇳 मुझे हर रोज़ office जाना पड़ता है।
🇬🇧 I **have to** go to office every day.

🇮🇳 उसे uniform पहनना पड़ता है।
🇬🇧 She **has to** wear a uniform.

**Negative (Don't have to = ज़रूरी नहीं):**
I don't have to = मुझे ज़रूरी नहीं (choice है!)
(यह "must not" से बिल्कुल अलग है — must not = absolutely forbidden)
rules: Negative: do not have to / does not have to (= not necessary, optional)
Question: Do I have to...? / Does she have to...?
Don't have to = optional (ज़रूरी नहीं, but you can if you want)
Have to = more common in spoken English than must
content.memoryTrick:
**Have To = RULE BOOK 📋** — जब कोई rule, boss, या situation कहे 'करो' = have to/has to.
**Don't have to = FREE CHOICE 🆓** — ज़रूरी नहीं, तुम्हारी मर्ज़ी!
Must Not ≠ Don't have to

examples.english: I have to wake up early tomorrow.

examples.hindi: उसे daily report submit करनी पड़ती है।
examples.english: He has to submit a daily report.
Do you have to go there?

examples.hindi: मुझे आज office नहीं जाना है। (छुट्टी है)
examples.english: I don't have to go to office today. (It's a holiday)

examples.hindi: हमें सभी rules follow करने पड़ते हैं।
examples.english: We have to follow all the rules.

mistakes.wrong: I don't have to go = I must not go.
mistakes.correct: Don't have to = optional; Must not = forbidden

mistakes.why: Does already adds the present tense — base form "have to" follows

speakingTips: अपनी daily obligations बताओ: 'I have to wake up at 6, I have to cook breakfast...'
Office में: 'I have to submit this report by end of day.'
Difference practice करो: 'I have to go.' (must) vs 'I don't have to go.' (optional)

vocabulary.example: Training is mandatory for new staff.
It is necessary to submit the form.
Attendance on Saturday is optional — you don't have to come.
Senior citizens are exempt from this rule.

practice.hindi: मुझे रोज़ ऑफिस जाना पड़ता है।
practice.english: I have to go to the office every day.
alternatives: i need to go to office daily.

practice.hindi: उसे दवाई खानी होगी।
practice.english: She has to take the medicine.
alternatives: she needs to take medicine.

practice.hindi: हमें समय पर काम खत्म करना होगा।
practice.english: We have to finish the work on time.
alternatives: we need to complete work on time.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम्हें आज काम करना है?
practice.english: Do you have to work today?
alternatives: do you need to work today?
practice.hint: Do you have to + base verb ?

practice.hindi: उसे कल परीक्षा देनी है।
practice.english: He has to give an exam tomorrow.
alternatives: he has to take an exam tomorrow.

practice.hindi: मुझे खाना बनाना पड़ेगा।
practice.english: I have to cook food.
alternatives: i need to make food.

practice.hindi: उन्हें नए नियम मानने होंगे।
practice.english: They have to follow the new rules.
alternatives: they need to obey the new rules.

practice.hindi: क्या उसे रोज़ दौड़ना पड़ता है?
practice.english: Does she have to run every day?
alternatives: does she need to run daily?
practice.hint: Does she have to + base verb ?

practice.hindi: मुझे नहीं जाना।
practice.english: I do not have to go.
alternatives: i don't have to go.
i need not go.
practice.hint: do not have to = no obligation

practice.hindi: वह यहाँ नहीं रहना चाहता लेकिन उसे रहना पड़ता है।
practice.english: He does not want to stay here but he has to.
alternatives: he doesn't want to stay but he has to.
practice.hint: does not want but has to

practice.hindi: हमें कड़ी मेहनत करनी है।
practice.english: We have to work hard.
alternatives: we need to work hard.

practice.hindi: उसे रोज़ स्कूल जाना पड़ता है।
practice.english: She has to go to school every day.
alternatives: she needs to attend school daily.

practice.hindi: मुझे डॉक्टर से मिलना है।
practice.english: I have to meet the doctor.
alternatives: i need to see the doctor.

practice.hindi: क्या हमें रात को यहाँ रुकना होगा?
practice.english: Do we have to stay here at night?
alternatives: do we need to stay overnight?

practice.hindi: उसे जल्दी उठना पड़ता है।
practice.english: He has to wake up early.
alternatives: he needs to get up early.

practice.hindi: मुझे यह रिपोर्ट आज जमा करनी है।
practice.english: I have to submit this report today.
alternatives: i need to hand in this report today.

practice.hindi: उन्हें सच बोलना पड़ेगा।
practice.english: They have to tell the truth.
alternatives: they must tell the truth.

practice.hindi: वह बिल्कुल नहीं जाना चाहती थी लेकिन उसे जाना था।
practice.english: She did not want to go at all but she had to.
alternatives: she didn't want to go but she had to.
practice.hint: did not want but had to

practice.hindi: क्या तुम्हें नई ड्रेस खरीदनी है?
practice.english: Do you have to buy a new dress?
alternatives: do you need to buy new clothes?

practice.hindi: इस नौकरी में बहुत यात्रा करनी पड़ती है।
practice.english: In this job, you have to travel a lot.
alternatives: this job requires a lot of travelling.

practice.hindi: वह कल ऑपरेशन के लिए जाने वाला है।
practice.english: He has to go for the operation tomorrow.
alternatives: he needs to go for surgery tomorrow.

practice.hindi: हमें अपनी गलतियों से सीखना होगा।
practice.english: We have to learn from our mistakes.
alternatives: we need to learn from our errors.

practice.hindi: उसे हर सुबह 5 बजे उठना पड़ता है।
practice.english: She has to wake up at 5 AM every morning.
alternatives: she needs to get up at 5 am daily.

practice.hindi: क्या बच्चों को स्कूल में यूनिफॉर्म पहननी पड़ती है?
practice.english: Do children have to wear uniforms at school?
alternatives: do kids have to wear school uniform?

practice.hindi: मुझे उससे माफ़ी माँगनी होगी।
practice.english: I have to apologize to him.
alternatives: i need to say sorry to him.
